evidence.page_title: Woodstock '94: Three More Days of Peace, Music…Drugs and Mud
evidence.title: Woodstock ’94: Three More Days of Peace, Music…Drugs and Mud
evidence.text:
When: August 12-14, 1994
Where: Winston Farm in Saugerties, New York (Approximately 100 miles of New York City)
Why: To celebrate the 25th anniversary of the original Woodstock, which was held in Bethal, New York, roughly 40 miles away from the 1994 festival.
Attendance: Approximately 350,000
The Lineup:
Aerosmith
Allman Brothers Band
Arrested Development
The Band
Blind Melon
Blues Traveler
Candlebox
Johnny Cash
Jimmy Cliff’s All Star Reggae Jam (with Rita Marley & Shabba Ranks)
Joe Cocker
Collective Soul
Counting Crows
The Cranberries
Crosby, Stills & Nash
Sheryl Crow
Country Joe McDonald (with the Fugs)
Cypress Hill
Del Amitri
Bob Dylan
Melissa Etheridge
Peter Gabriel
Green Day
Jackyl
King’s X
Live
Metallica
Neville Brothers
Nine Inch Nails
Orleans
Paul Rodgers Band (with Neal Schon and Andy Fraser)
Porno for Pyros
Primus
Red Hot Chili Peppers
Rollins Band
Todd Rundgren
Salt-N-Pepa
Santana
Sisters of Glory
Spin Doctors
Sweetwater
Traffic
Violent Femmes
Youssou N’Dour
Zucchero
As a geek for everything 90’s, I decided to take a look at an event that helped define the decade in a strong way. Instead of going through a timeline of events that occurred that weekend in upstate New York, I decided to highlight the top performances by bands and artists from the festival. Off we go with three more days of peace, music and my Top Five Performances of Woodstock 1994.
Honorable mentions:
Blues Traveler, Joe Cocker, Cypress Hill, Allman Brothers Band
5. Crosby, Stills & Nash
A throwback to three gentlemen who performed at the original Woodstock 25 years prior. If there was anybody who could compete with the current music scene it were these three old school hippies. As they took the stage on Saturday afternoon, the temperature in Saugerties was soaring into the mid 80’s. Many fans in attendance were well intoxicated from their drug and or alcohol of choice. CSN opened their set with the very fitting, Love the One You’re With. For the next hour and a half, classic tunes such as, “Deja Vu”, “Southern Cross” and “Long Time Gone” were performed by CSN. In addition, the fellas covered The Beatles, “In My Life” and Buffalo Springfield’s, “For What It’s Worth” The crowd wasn’t disappointed as the mainly 18-25 year old crowd was treated to a nice throwback to an era of music that was an influence to their parents and the generation prior. David Crosby, Steven Stills and Graham Nash ended their set the only way it could. The performed, “Woodstock”. A song originally written by Joni Mitchell, but later covered by these three gentlemen.
4. Blind Melon
Drugs weren’t just among the nearly 350,000 people in attendance, but were also well taken advantage of by the artists on stage. Exhibit A, Blind Melon frontman, Shannon Hoon. Hoon took the stage on Saturday afternoon on the festival’s North stage in his wife’s wedding dress and looked to be in a world of his own. The reason, a lethal combination of LSD and mushrooms. However, Melon were on their A game that afternoon. One of the most underrated bands of the early nineties brought their amazing mix of alternative rock, folk and jam band to upstate New York and performed a 45 minute set that was a true highlight of the three-day event. The set included Melon tunes, “Tones of Home”, “I Wonder”, “Deserted” and their huge radio hit, “No Rain”. Sadly, Hoon would pass away just over a year later on October 21, 1995 due to a cocaine overdose. Hoon and Blind Melon’s legacy was cut short do to drug addiction, but their performance during this festival will never be forgotten.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sPE8yUDZjOY
3. Primus
Oh boy, where to begin. The southern California alternative-funk band are just were simply amazing. As the band took the South Stage on Saturday afternoon, the hundreds of thousands in attendance were in for a treat. The band’s frontman, Les Claypool isn’t only the voice of the band , but also an unbelievably talented bass player. Next to Rush’s Getty Lee, Claypool might be the most talented vocalist/bassist of all time. The bands set opened with the very suitable, “To Defy the Laws of Tradition”. For the next hour the band killed it on stage with a set that also included, “Here Come the Bastards”, “Bob” and “My name is Mud”, which was a little foreshadowing of things that would occur later that evening. The highlight of their set was a cameo appearance by Alice in Chains guitarist, Jerry Cantrell as he joined the band on stage to perform, “Harold on the Rocks”. Alice in Chains was originally scheduled to perform at the festival, but do to the on going drug addiction of frontman, Layne Staley, the band declined to appear. Before Primus left the stage, they had one more song to play as they covered, Metallica’s “Master of Puppets”. Which leads me into my next selection of top performances from the weekend.
2. Metallica
For anyone who has seen the band live over their 30 year career, you know that when, Ennio Morricone’s, “The Ecstacey of Gold” plays, shit is about to get crazy. The bands signature intro before taking the stage echoed through the crowd of the North Stage just past 11pm on Saturday night. As the four horsemen of metal took the stage, everyone in attendance was about to experience the loudest performance of the weekend. They opened their set with a cover of Bungie’s “Breadfan”, a song usually reserved for the bands encore performances. After that, it was an hour and forty-five minuyes of classic ‘Tallica tunes including, “For Whom the Bell Tolls”, “Fade to Black”, “Creeping Death and “One”. James, Lars, Kirk and Jason give it their all every show and play off their die hard crowds every time. This evening was no different as Saugerties, New York found out exactly way this band from the Bay Area are the God’s of thrash metal.
1. Nine Inch Nails
On a hot and humid afternoon in Saugerties, the rain began to fall from the sky onto 350,000 people. Sure it was a nice way to cool off for a little bit, but along with rain came mud and lots of it. The area that was being used for the festival was farm land and by mid-afternoon on Saturday, tie-dye turned to a brown mess. Thousands of people in attendance took advantage of the sloppy mess as they began moshing, sliding and rolling in the mud left from the storms. Among those who participated were Trent Reznor and the rest of NIN. By the time they took the stage, the industrial metal/rock boys were filthy and wet, but they didn’t give a flying fuck as they were about to put on the best performance of the weekend. For the next hour and a half, NIN proved why they were one of the most influential groups of the decade. The band’s set included, “Terrible Lie”, “Closer”, “Down in It” and ‘Wish” among others. The NIN set from that evening was exactly what this festival needed to make it a more unique event than the festival 25 years prior.
I hope everyone enjoyed a look back at this memorable weekend. 20 years later, this event still holds up as one of the best concert festivals of all time. I’ll see everyone again in 2019 when I take a look back at Woodstock ’99.
image_captions: Some of the 350,000 on hand in Saugerties, New York
MTV’s Riki Rachtman chats backstage with Metallica’s Lars Ulrich and James Hetfield

evidence.page_title: Throwback Thursday: The Tragic Truth About Woodstock '99
evidence.title: Throwback Thursday: The Tragic Truth About Woodstock '99
evidence.description: Sixteen years ago, followed by violence, rape and unequivocal chaos, Woodstock went down in flames.
evidence.text:
Sixteen years ago, followed by violence, rape and unequivocal chaos, Woodstock Festival went down in flames.
On July 22, 1999, what was supposed to be a sort of 30th anniversary tribute to the original Woodstock ’69, digressed into a chaotic hellhole that harboured violence, crime and nightmare-like mayhem. Woodstock ’69’s whole quintessential agenda was ‘love, peace and happiness’ which was promptly obliterated in the ’99 edition of the festival.
How it began.
It all started with John Scher, the festival promoter who’d lost his money courtesy Woodstock ’94 telling the reporters, “You can have a Woodstock, and it can be a safe and secure environment. We’re going to try and make a profit on this one.”
There are several factors that contributed to the imminent decomposition of the festival that year – poor organization that resulted in despicable and barbaric behavior being a substantial one. It is said that the festival was held on hot tarmac ground in late-July heat – temperature running over a 100 degrees Fahrenheit – with potentially no shaded areas, there were alarmingly low number of toilets, the festival crew/security was low-paid, the lineup sets were poorly scheduled and the anonymity that comes with being in a crowd let the now sweaty, angry, aggressive festival goers out of hand, resulting in not one, but several sexual assault reports and even a few lawsuits.
The fires.
“Suddenly, we saw some activity a few feet in front of us. A bunch of people were gathering up some garbage in a big pile. We looked at each other. We knew what it meant.” says Jeremie Romain, a Woodstock ’99 survivor, in this article.
The shit hit the ceiling when the Red Hot Chili Peppers unleashed a cover of Jimi Hendrix’s “Fire” during their festival-closing set. This ensued an array of adrenaline driven tools to light bonfires on festival grounds by burning garbage. Vendor booths, personal belongings stolen from tents and even vehicles were set on fire and used as fuel for the lit bonfires. This was when all hedonistic hell broke loose, when the already blurry line between the acceptable and the inexplicable dissipated altogether.
And while Red Hot Chili Peppers inadvertently riled up the crowd, the same can’t be said about Fred Durst, Limp Bizkit’s frontman. Durst, quite thoughtlessly, we might add, to an already wild crowd proclaimed, “People are getting hurt. Don’t let anybody get hurt. But I don’t think you should mellow out. That’s what Alanis Morissette had you motherfuckers do. If someone falls, pick ’em up. We already let the negative energy out. Now we wanna let out the positive energy”
The stampede and the literal shit-fights.
“The mud pile that was created the day before by festival goers breaking the water pipes next to the port-a-potties had turned into a battlefield. People were throwing mud left and right. When my friend got hit in the back and we got a whiff of it, we knew it wasn’t mud.” says Jeremie Romain.
Overcrowding was one of the biggest reasons for the downfall of the festival. And given the unfriendly festival environment, it was no surprise that even the attendees not displaying aggressive behavior were thoroughly frustrated with how the festival was progressing. Once the fires started, there was chaos everywhere.
People struggled through the gargantuan mass of other people to get to their tents, only to find most of them robbed. Around this time is when the overflowing port-a-potties were turned over, thrown into fires, and we witnessed the boneheadedness people now speak of as shit-fights were instigated.
The sexual assaults.
“At one point I saw this girl, a very petite girl, maybe 100 pounds, who was body-surfing above the crowd and either fell in or was pulled into a circle in the mosh pit,” David Schneider, 28, told MTV after the festival in ’99. “These gentlemen, probably in the 25–32 age range, looked as though they were holding her down. They were holding her arms; you could see she was struggling. You could see he was violating her. Then it looked like he passed her off to his friend next to him.
“It looked like a clear gang-rape to me, where he was just passing her on to at least one other person. At some point, it appears she gave up struggling.”
A minimum of four confirmed rapes were reported after the festival was over. What was even worse were the sexual assaults that went unreported.
“It turned into complete and total anarchy by the second night of the festival. And that gang rape in the pit during Limp Bizkit’s set wasn’t the only gang rape that happened at that fest.” says a festival attendee on a Reddit forum.
What compelled seemingly normal, festival attending individuals to commit such sadistic acts? The combination of heat, readily available drugs and alcohol, and the lack of food and sleep is the “perfect breeding ground for sexual assault” says crisis services director of the YWCA, Rosemary Vennero.
Or is it simply the invisibility provided by such a large crowd, which deems an individual unrecognizable? As disturbing as the thought is, it may be a viable explanation for unlikely crowd behavior seen in many such cases. Explore more about rape culture and music festivals here.
The aftermath.
State police said 44 arrests were made during the three-day festival weekend. Woodstock organizers said about 1,200 people were treated each day at on-site medical facilities. Rome Memorial Hospital would not release information on specific cases but reported that it treated 123 Woodstock attendees.
Apart from this, numerous sexual assault reports came up, along with lawsuits filed by attendees that were injured and/or involuntarily swallowed up in the madness during the festival. The legacy of the greatest outdoor music festival that essentially propagated peace and love was literally burned down to ashes thirty years later with an abominable, apocalyptic tribute infested with anarchy, arson and riots.
We suppose Woodstock ’99 will always be considered one of the greatest tragedies in music festival history. Now you know why.
Also, click here to view The Sherp’s list of cringe-worthy festival disasters, including Woodstock ’99.

evidence.page_title: Heavy Metal Concerts That Shook the World: Their Legacies Live On
evidence.title: Heavy Metal Concerts That Shook the World: Their Legacies Live On
evidence.description: Heavy metal music has a rich history filled with electrifying performances that have left audiences awestruck. From the iconic festivals to legendary band tours, each concert has contributed to the legacy of this enduring genre. In this blog post, we’ll take you through some of the most famous heavy metal concerts, explore their legacies, and discuss how they continue to shape the world of music today. Whether you’re a fan of new albums or enthusiastic about concert photography, this article...
evidence.text:
Heavy Metal Concerts That Shook the World: Their Legacies Live On
- May 20, 2025
- 5 min read
Heavy metal music has a rich history filled with electrifying performances that have left audiences awestruck. From the iconic festivals to legendary band tours, each concert has contributed to the legacy of this enduring genre. In this blog post, we’ll take you through some of the most famous heavy metal concerts, explore their legacies, and discuss how they continue to shape the world of music today. Whether you’re a fan of new albums or enthusiastic about concert photography, this article is sure to appeal to your interests.
The Birth of a Genre: Woodstock 1994
While not often classified solely as a heavy metal concert, Woodstock 1994 introduced many to the heavier side of rock. With bands like Metallica and Slayer taking center stage, the vibe shifted toward a darker, more rebellious tone. The festival encapsulated what heavy metal music stands for: freedom of expression and the power to challenge societal norms.
Impact: Heavy metal has since found a secure spot in the festival scene.
Legacy: Woodstock became a model for bands to showcase their new albums to a massive audience.
Monsters of Rock 1988
Held at Donington Park in England, Monsters of Rock 1988 is one of the most iconic heavy metal concerts in history. Featuring legendary acts such as Iron Maiden and KISS, this concert attracted over 100,000 fans. The atmosphere was electric, and the lineup was a dream come true for any heavy metal aficionado.
The concert not only set the stage for future events but also pushed the limits of stage production, creating what we know today as spectacular concert experiences. This is where concert review culture truly began to flourish, as fans started documenting their experiences and sharing them online.
Live Aid 1985: The Power of Rock for a Cause
While primarily aimed at raising awareness for the Ethiopian famine, Live Aid saw heavy metal bands like Metallica and Black Sabbath contribute to the rock lineup. The collaboration of different genres at this concert showcased how heavy metal music could unite varying styles for a greater good.
This historical performance emphasized the importance of music events not just as entertainment but also as avenues for social change. The iconic moment of Phil Collins performing at both Wembley and JFK Stadium redefined what a concert could be, proving to the world the global reach of music.
Download Festival: A Heavy Metal Mecca
Since its inception in 2003, the Download Festival has become synonymous with heavy metal music. Each year, fans gather to witness the greatness of bands old and new. The festival has served as a launching pad for many bands’ new albums, making it a crucial event for both legacy acts and emerging talents.
Notable Performances: From Slipknot to Ozzy Osbourne, the variety boasts performances that cater to all sub-genres within heavy metal.
Impact on Music News: Many significant music news publications cover the festival, solidifying its place as a ‘must-attend’ event.
Ozzfest: The Birth of Metal Festivals
Ozzfest, created by Ozzy Osbourne, has been pivotal in not just promoting heavy metal music but also launching the careers of numerous bands. Starting in 1996, this tour brought together established acts and fresh talent, allowing fans to explore a rich tapestry of heavy sounds.
The festival has influenced countless imitators, but its legacy remains unique due to the strong association with its creator. Ozzy himself was a constant reminder of the roots of heavy metal music, often releasing a new album around the same time as the tours.
Unforgettable Moments: The Big Four in 2010
The Big Four concert in 2010—comprising Metallica, Slayer, Megadeth, and Anthrax—was a significant event for thrash metal fans. Hosted in several locations, including Sofia, Bulgaria, this concert marked a historic gathering that showcased the camaraderie between the four bands.
Not only did this concert serve as a reunion for fans who had followed each band throughout their careers, but it also marked a pivotal moment in heavy metal history by introducing younger generations to classic acts. The night was highlighted by stunning performances that provided photographers with once-in-a-lifetime opportunities for concert photography.
Voodoo Music + Arts Experience: A Heavy Metal Blend
Although Voodoo has a diverse roster that spans multiple genres, the presence of heavy metal acts like Metallica and Tool cannot be overlooked. This festival is renowned for its eclectic variety, attracting fans from various musical backgrounds.
With its unique setting in New Orleans, Voodoo has successfully created an atmosphere where heavy metal fans can enjoy the rich cultural tapestry of the city along with thrilling performances. These dynamic offerings are perfect for those who thrive on the latest in music news.
Download Germany: An Emerging Icon
As an extension of the UK festival, Download Germany has emerged as a significant player in the heavy metal circuit since its start in 2016. Featuring major headliners and local talent, it’s quickly garnered a following among European heavy metal fans. With multiple stages and a wide array of genres, it has effectively become a melting pot for heavy metal enthusiasts.
Artists: Performers range from classic metal bands to modern giants, ensuring a diverse lineup.
Documentation: The festival encourages concert reviews, enhancing the post-festival narrative and building excitement for future events.
A Legacy That Endures
Every historic concert mentioned has contributed to the rich tapestry of heavy metal music. They’ve defined eras and created legends while also paving the way for future generations. The influence of heavy metal remains relevant, as newer bands draw inspiration from the giants of the past, creating new sounds and styles that honor their roots.
As the tradition of heavy metal concerts continues, the public will always have an eye on significant events to catch the next big band or a legendary reunion. New albums are always on the horizon, and each performance could become a chapter in music history. The loyal community of heavy metal fans ensures that the spirit of these concerts carries on.
Join the Heavy Metal Revolution
If you’re already part of the heavy metal community, you know the adrenaline that comes with attending live concerts. If you haven’t yet experienced it, there’s never been a better time to dive into the chaos and energy of a heavy metal concert environment. Make sure to stay updated on your favorite bands through music news platforms, and keep an eye out for the next festival that speaks to you.
Embrace the connection that comes with live music, look for local shows, or plan a trip to a festival that showcases your favorite acts. Share your experiences through concert photography or concert reviews to further contribute to the legacy of heavy metal music. This is not just a genre; it’s a culture that welcomes anyone ready to be part of something extraordinary.
So, grab your leather jacket and turn up the volume—the world of heavy metal concerts is waiting for you. Let the legacy of these iconic performances inspire your own journey through this powerful and electrifying musical genre!
FAQs
What are some iconic heavy metal concerts discussed in the blog?
The blog discusses several iconic heavy metal concerts including Woodstock 1994, Monsters of Rock 1988, Live Aid 1985, and Ozzfest.
How did Woodstock 1994 influence heavy metal music?
Woodstock 1994 introduced many to the heavier side of rock and helped solidify heavy metal's presence in the festival scene, allowing bands to showcase their new albums to a large audience.
What is the significance of the Download Festival?
The Download Festival, established in 2003, has become synonymous with heavy metal music, serving as a platform for both legendary acts and emerging talent to perform.
What was the Big Four concert in 2010?
The Big Four concert in 2010 featured Metallica, Slayer, Megadeth, and Anthrax, marking a historic gathering for thrash metal fans and introducing younger generations to classic acts.
How can fans contribute to the legacy of heavy metal concerts?
Fans can contribute to the legacy by sharing their experiences through concert photography and reviews, staying updated on music events, and participating in local shows or festivals.

evidence.page_title: S-EYE VIEW700 Arthur Parker Ave, Woodstock - Buya Bamba Limited
evidence.title: Buya Bamba Limited
evidence.text:
We are focused on growing only the best
Everything Buya Bamba does is focused on growing the best, and our dedication has made us the leading distributor of table and processing potatoes in Zambia.
We specialise in superior seed supply, growing, harvesting, cleaning, sizing and packaging, as well as, the storage of fresh potatoes to provide our customers with a dependable source of local produce on a year-round basis.
We ensure that home-grown produce meets world-class standards through our partnership with leading global potato industry players.
We are regionally competitive in terms of quality, products and price through maintaining consistency in market supply and meeting demand.
Our Services
01
Planting & Harvesting
Buya Bamba is the premium partner of GRIMME in Zambia. GRIMME offers the most comprehensive and widest product range…
02
Handling & Agro-Processing
Our exceptional packing and handling facilities allows Buya Bamba to supply our product for any agro-processing requirements…
03
Logistics, Distribution & Outlets
For easy accessibility to our product lines, our footprint now reaches Copperbelt, Southern, Central and Northern provinces…
04
Seed Varieties
We supply exceptional seed varieties best suited for Zambian conditions to yield top quality produce…
Buya Bamba has been in operation since 1999.
Initially formed to provide washed, fresh potatoes to the local market, we are now a major and essential contributor to the diversification of agriculture in Zambia.
Giving back to the Community
We are more than a produce supplier. Buya Bamba also believes in nurturing our local community as much as our potatoes and onions.
Quality Seed
Our seed varieties are chosen with the utmost care to ensure sufficient crop yields that are sustainable for both the Zambian ecosystem and economy.
Buying Network
Our informal buying network helps to empower people to become entrepreneurs, ensuring that they provide affordable and nutritional produce to the community, and also uplift themselves.
Market Availability
The combined world class storage capabilities of Buya Bamba and our commercial growers allow us to provide the market with excellent produce at affordable prices throughout the year.
Planting to Distribution
From planting and production, to harvesting & handling, Buya Bamba and our commercial growers help provide job opportunities, especially in the rural areas, where there is a much needed boost for economic prosperity.
Quality supplied across the potato value chain
We specialise in superior seed supply, growing, harvesting, cleaning, sizing and packaging, as well as, the storage of fresh potatoes to provide our customers with a dependable, world-class source of local produce on a year-round basis.
Buya Bamba Distribution
Our products are available nationally at food outlets throughout Zambia.
Our ware products are packed to a 10kg standard pocket size but can also be packed to smaller sizes on order from 500g bags to 5kg bags for resellers.
OFFICE ADDRESS
Nampundwe Road
Chinika, Lusaka
PHONE NUMBER
+26 (0211) 286942/286315
